methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Fervo Energy is a Climate & Energy sector company at the IPO stage, founded by Tim Latimer and Jack Norbeck, having raised $1.5B+ to date. The comparable universe consists of 1 public company (LNZA) and 8 private peers including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape. This modest peer set reflects the nascent nature of frontier energy technologies.

Standard peer-median valuation analysis is not applicable here. The Climate & Energy sector contains 19 same-sector companies, but zero meet the filtering thresholds for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comparables. The minimum threshold of three datapoints required for robust peer-median triangulation cannot be satisfied. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion and is therefore rejected.

Given the data constraints, this analysis cannot support a triangulated valuation range grounded in comparable multiples. Fervo's IPO pricing will depend on investor appetite, market conditions at launch, and underwriter positioning relative to the fragmented peer set. Key uncertainties include the degree to which public-market investors will price frontier energy risk relative to late-stage private funding; the valuation precedent set by LNZA; and whether Fervo commands a premium or discount versus the private cohort given its stage and capital raise. A formal range would require either expanded comps, transaction precedent data, or DCF analysis—none of which are provided here.
